description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what caveat is documented for CMF?
Feature Caveat: CMF - No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

description: When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

description: Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what caveat is documented for RADIUS?
Feature Caveat: RADIUS - Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: Symptom: FQDN radius-server does not resolve to the respective Host IP and tracking flags it as unreachable. Scenario: This issue may occur when RADIUS servers are configured using FQDN hostnames rather than IP addresses and RADIUS tracking is enabled after a switch reboot. Workaround: Remove and re-add the RADIUS server configuration after each reboot. Restart the radius tracking process after each reboot. Configure static DNS host entries for the RADIUS servers in the switch configuration. Use IP addresses instead of FQDN hostnames for RADIUS servers. Force restart radius-srv-trkd process for the quick fix.

description: Symptom: When switching between IPTV channels, there is an intermittent delay of 2-6 seconds before the new video stream is displayed to the end user. Scenario: The system experiences a small delay in the program of a new multicast flow after receiving the IGMP report. During this time, critical initial video packets containing SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) and PPS (Picture Parameter Set) information may be dropped. These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream. Workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams as follows: static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port> This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency. configure interface IP and Active gateway IP/MAC when interface is UP and running.

description: Symptom: Inaccurate SNMP reading for MIB ifHCInOctets. Scenario: SNMP IF-MIB counters may not be accurate for LAG ports. CLI is not impacted by this issue and represents LAG ports counters correctly. configure interface IP and Active gateway IP/MAC when interface is UP and running.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what is the workaround for Counters Bug 353229?
Counters (Bug ID 353229): No workaround is documented in the release notes.

description: Symptom: Switches may fail to respond to SNMP queries after upgrading from 10.08 version of code to 10.13 code. Scenario: This issue occurs when a switch has been configured with snmp-server response-source prior to upgrade. Workaround: After upgrading, access the switch shell and either: 1. Delete the file /fs/nos/net-snmp/agent-swns/snmp.conf, or 2. Edit the file to comment out (with #) the line containing [snmp] srcipaddr entry 3. Restart the SNMP service with systemctl restart snmpd-wrapper-in-c.service

description: Symptom: The switch intermittently fails to include the Framed-IP attribute in RADIUS accounting packets. This results in missing IP address information for authenticated clients. Scenario: The issue is observed when a client disconnects and reconnects after a short interval (for example, 90 seconds). The Framed-IP attribute may be missing even though the client has a valid IP address and is visible in other system outputs. Workaround: Ensure continuous client traffic after reconnection to allow the switch to relearn the IP address. Alternatively, re-enable MAC authentication on the port or perform a shut/no shut cycle to restore expected behavior.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX.
scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features.
workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: NTP entries are not updating correctly with dhcpv4 and v6 client enable/disable Scenario 1: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the same VLAN with DHCPv4 enabled and DHCPv6 disabled: Disabling DHCPv4 and enabling DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected When both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the same VLAN with DHCPv4 disabled and DHCPv6 enabled: Enabling DHCPv4 and disabling DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected Scenario 2: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the different VLANs: Re-enabling DHCP mode on lower interface VLAN does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected
symptom: NTP entries are not updating correctly with dhcpv4 and v6 client enable/disable
scenario: 2: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the different VLANs: Re-enabling DHCP mode on lower interface VLAN does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected

section: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
task: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
procedure: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy).
Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config.
Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what is the manual configuration restore procedure for software downgrade?

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

procedure: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.

Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.

When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

messages.content: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, how do you perform the software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
